name: Cheese Enchilada Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 10 oz can enchilada sauce (Old El Paso, mild)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, stir together the cream of mushroom soup and enchilada sauce.
2. Grease a 9x13-inch baking pan.
3. Tear several corn tortillas into bite-sized pieces (about 8 pieces each) and line the bottom of the pan with one layer of tortilla pieces, overlapping slightly to avoid any open spots.
4. Gently spread one-third of the soup-sauce mixture on top of the tortilla pieces.
5. Sprinkle one-third of the cheese over the top of the sauce.
6. Sprinkle one-third of the chopped onions and bell peppers over the top of the cheese.
7. Repeat layers two more times, for a total of three layers.
8. Bake at 350°F until the cheese is bubbly and the onions and bell peppers on top are cooked and turning brown on the edges but not burned (about 30 minutes).
9. For freezing: Line the pan with foil, grease the foil, build the casserole, cook for a shorter period until the cheese is melted but not bubbly, let cool, cut into halves, package each half separately, wrap in foil, and place in freezer bags.
10. To reheat, place frozen casserole on foil or plastic wrap in a pan and cook at 350°F until heated through.
11. Serve with Spanish rice (cooked brown rice mixed with stewed tomatoes) and canned fat-free "refried" beans.
